name: Most Dangerous Addiction — Unexpected Wordplay
technique: misdirection
platforms: [x]
tags: [wordplay, misdirection, unexpected-addiction, polarizing, single-sentence]
source_month: "2024-11"
---

## Why It Works

This works for a few reasons:
- "The most dangerous addiction…" is an opening line that plays into the content X likes.
- I flip it on its head by not talking about common addictions (drugs/alcohol)
- "People who don't matter." is a harsh ending that makes people lean in and participate.

## Template

The most dangerous addiction in [INDUSTRY] is [WORDPLAY ON SOMETHING THAT ISN'T A COMMON ADDICTION].

## Example

The most dangerous addiction in fitness is the obsession with short-term transformations.
